ocr.text: In Q1 2024, HSBC Holdings reported key metrics indicating a total revenue of HKD1.5B, net income of HKD600M, and
operating costs of HKD100M, resulting in total expenses of HKD2.5B and a gross profit of HKD900M.
KeyMetrics  Q12024  TotalRevenue  NetIncome  OperatingCosts
RevenueBreakdown  InterestIncome  Fees&Commissions  TradingIncome  TotalExpenses
GrossProfit  EBITDA  NetProfitMargin  ReturnonAssets  ReturnonEquity  KeyRatios
HKD1.5B  HKD600M  HKD300M  HKD100M  HKD2.5B  HKD900M
HKD150M  HKD200M  15.0%  2.5%  12.0%  NetIncomeGrowth
20%  Cost-to-Income  45%  Tier1Capital  12.5%  LiquidityRatio
150%  Loan-to-Deposit  80%  MarketShare  25%  CustomerDeposits
HKD10B  CommercialLoans  HKD8B  RetailLoans  HKD4B  InvestmentPortfolio
HKD5B  Risk-WeightedAssets  HKD40B  TotalAssets  HKD80B  TotalLiabilities
The revenue breakdown showcased interest income of HKD150M, fees and commissions of HKD200M, and trading income of
HKD300M, leading to an EBITDA of HKD900M.
Non-PerformingLoansOverview  Q12024  HKD
Category  Amount  Ratio  Status
CorporateLoans  300M  2.5%  UnderReview
RetailLoans  150M  1.8%  Stable
Mortgages  200M  1.6%  Improving
SmallBusiness  100M  3.0%  Critical
TotalNPLs  850M  2.2%  OverallStatus
The net profit margin stood at 15.0%, return on assets at 2.5%, and return on equity at 12.0%, with net income growth at 20%.
Key ratios revealed a cost-to-income ratio of 45%, tier 1 capital of 12.5%, liquidity ratio at 150%, loan-to-deposit ratio of 80%,
market share at 25%, and customer deposits totaling HKD10B. Commercial loans amounted to HKD5B, retail loans to HKD8B,
and the investment portfolio reached HKD4B, while risk-weighted assets were HKD5B, total assets at HKD40B, and total
liabilities at HKD80B. In the non-performing loans overview, corporate loans were valued at HKD300M with a ratio of 2.5%
and status as under review, retail loans at HKD150M with a ratio of 1.8% and stable status, mortgages at HKD200M with a
ratio of 1.6% and improving status, small business loans at HKD100M with a ratio of 3.0% and critical status, and total NPLs
reaching HKD850M with an overall status of 2.2%.
